tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P1] Shared command-intent registry + resolver + subpath export + tests
tasks.description: Create packages/shared/src/command-intents.ts — a pure, dependency-free module that is the single source of truth for the three canonical command intents and their cross-agent aliases. Export (TSDoc on every export, Hard Rule 4): (a) type CommandIntentId = 'compact' | 'clear' | 'context'; (b) type CommandIntentFulfillment = 'runtime' | 'client-native'; (c) type RuntimeCommandIntentId = Extract<CommandIntentId, 'compact'>; (d) interface CommandIntentDescriptor { id: CommandIntentId; canonical: string; description: string; argumentHint?: string; aliases: readonly string[]; fulfillment: CommandIntentFulfillment }; (e) const COMMAND_INTENTS: readonly CommandIntentDescriptor[] with EXACTLY three entries — compact (canonical '/compact', description 'Shrink the conversation to free up context', aliases ['/compress','/summarize'], fulfillment 'runtime'), clear (canonical '/clear', description 'Start a fresh session in this project', aliases ['/new','/new-chat'], fulfillment 'client-native'), context (canonical '/context', description 'Show context usage and cost', aliases ['/usage','/cost','/stats','/status'], fulfillment 'client-native'); (f) function resolveCommandIntent(token: string): CommandIntentDescriptor | null — trim the token, prepend '/' if missing, lowercase, and return the descriptor whose canonical OR any alias matches case-insensitively, else null; (g) function commandIntentTokens(): ReadonlySet<string> — the set of every canonical + alias token (lowercased, '/'-prefixed) for the palette dedupe pass. Add the subpath export './command-intents' to the exports map in packages/shared/package.json following the existing pattern ({ types: './src/command-intents.ts', default: './dist/command-intents.js' }), and re-export the public types/values from the types barrel packages/shared/src/types.ts. Write packages/shared/src/__tests__/command-intents.test.ts (each case a purpose comment): resolveCommandIntent maps every canonical token and every alias, case-insensitive, with and without a leading '/', to the correct intent; returns null for unknown tokens and for near-misses like '/summarizefoo'; commandIntentTokens contains every canonical + alias and nothing else; each descriptor's fulfillment is correct (compact=runtime, clear/context=client-native). Files: packages/shared/src/command-intents.ts, packages/shared/package.json, packages/shared/src/types.ts, packages/shared/src/__tests__/command-intents.test.ts. Acceptance: module imports via '@dorkos/shared/command-intents'; all unit tests pass. Verify: pnpm vitest run packages/shared/src/__tests__/command-intents.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 1 (Shared registry).
tasks.activeForm: Building the shared command-intent registry and resolver

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P1] Add required RuntimeCapabilities.commandIntents to interface + all four caps constants + FakeAgentRuntime (atomic)
tasks.description: COMPILE-SAFETY ATOMIC TASK: commandIntents is a REQUIRED field, so the interface change and every capabilities producer must land in this ONE task or the workspace is left red. In packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts (RuntimeCapabilities interface, ~line 242) add, as a first-class sibling of permissionModes (NOT the features bag — ADR-0256): export interface CommandIntentSupport { supported: boolean } and a required field commandIntents: Record<RuntimeCommandIntentId, CommandIntentSupport> with TSDoc (import RuntimeCommandIntentId from './command-intents'; note supported:false means the palette disables the entry 'Not supported by {runtime}' and the composer refuses to send it as text). In the SAME task add the field to all four caps constants so every package compiles — set each to commandIntents: { compact: { supported: false } } as the Phase-1 placeholder (Phase 2 flips claude-code/opencode/test-mode to true alongside their real fulfillment bodies; codex stays false permanently): CLAUDE_CODE_CAPABILITIES (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/runtime-constants.ts:21), OPENCODE_CAPABILITIES (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/runtime-constants.ts:35), CODEX_CAPABILITIES (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/runtime-constants.ts:34), TEST_MODE_CAPABILITIES (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/runtime-constants.ts:25). Also add commandIntents: { compact: { supported: true } } to FakeAgentRuntime.getCapabilities (packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts) — the test double is the one supported runtime that Phase-1 conformance (task 1.5) exercises for the supported path. Grep for a paired Zod schema RuntimeCapabilitiesSchema: none exists as of 2026-07-16, so no OpenAPI schema edit is needed — the GET /api/capabilities route serializes the plain object automatically (verified: apps/server/src/routes/capabilities.ts). Files: packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts, the four runtime-constants.ts, packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts. Acceptance: shared + server + test-utils typecheck pass; the field is required (deleting it from any constant is a type error). Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/test-utils typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 2 (RuntimeCapabilities.commandIntents), ADR-0256.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the required commandIntents capability across every producer

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P1] Add required AgentRuntime.executeCommandIntent to interface + implement on all five runtimes (atomic)
tasks.description: COMPILE-SAFETY ATOMIC TASK: executeCommandIntent is a REQUIRED method, so the interface addition and every implementor must land together. In packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts, near sendMessage/getCapabilities, add: executeCommandIntent(sessionId: string, intent: RuntimeCommandIntentId, opts?: MessageOpts): AsyncGenerator<StreamEvent> with TSDoc stating it fulfills a RUNTIME-fulfilled intent (currently compact) by expanding the neutral intent into the runtime's native mechanism (ADR-0273), yields the resulting StreamEvents so the server drives them through the durable session projector exactly like a turn (the client learns of the compaction via GET /api/sessions/:id/events, e.g. compact_boundary), and is called ONLY when getCapabilities().commandIntents[intent].supported. Implement on: FakeAgentRuntime (packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts) — an async generator that yields a synthetic compact_boundary StreamEvent so conformance + e2e can assert dispatch reached the runtime (this is its FINAL form); and the four production runtime classes under apps/server/src/services/runtimes/{claude-code,opencode,codex,test-mode}/ — add the method as a compile-safe PLACEHOLDER that throws a typed unsupported error (e.g. throw new Error("executeCommandIntent(compact) not yet wired for <runtime>")). These placeholders are internally consistent with the supported:false values from task 1.2 (nothing calls them yet — the route lands in Phase 2 task 2.4). Phase 2 replaces the claude-code/opencode/test-mode placeholders with real bodies and flips their caps to true; codex's throw becomes its final typed unsupported error (task 2.3). Files: packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts, packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts, apps/server/src/services/runtimes/{claude-code,opencode,codex,test-mode}/ runtime classes. Acceptance: shared + server + test-utils typecheck pass; FakeAgentRuntime.executeCommandIntent('compact') yields a compact_boundary. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/test-utils typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 3 (AgentRuntime.executeCommandIntent). Sequential after 1.2 (both edit agent-runtime.ts and fake-agent-runtime.ts).
tasks.activeForm: Adding executeCommandIntent to the interface and every runtime

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P1] Add Transport.runCommandIntent to interface + HttpTransport + DirectTransport
tasks.description: Add the client-facing transport method so all four client surfaces share one path to trigger a runtime-fulfilled intent. In packages/shared/src/transport.ts (Transport interface, ~line 281) add: runCommandIntent(sessionId: string, intent: RuntimeCommandIntentId): Promise<{ sessionId: string }> with TSDoc (import RuntimeCommandIntentId from './command-intents'). Implement in both concrete transports so the interface compiles: HttpTransport (apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/http-transport.ts) — POST to `/api/sessions/${sessionId}/command-intents/${intent}` and return the parsed { sessionId } (mirror the existing trigger-only message POST helper already in this file). The server route lands in Phase 2, so a live call 404s until then — expected; it does not block compilation or client unit tests (which mock the transport). DirectTransport (apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct-transport.ts and/or its direct/*.ts submodules) — add the method as a compile-safe placeholder that throws 'not yet wired'; the in-process server service it will call is built in Phase 2 task 2.4, modeled on apps/server/src/services/session/embedded-turn-trigger.ts. Files: packages/shared/src/transport.ts, apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/http-transport.ts, apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct-transport.ts. Acceptance: shared + client typecheck pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 4 (Transport + route). Depends on 1.1 for RuntimeCommandIntentId; runs in parallel with 1.3 (different files).

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P1] Extend the runtime conformance suite for command intents
tasks.description: Extend the shared runtime conformance suite so every runtime is checked for the command-intent surface. In packages/test-utils/src/runtime-conformance.ts extend the capabilities assertions to: (a) assert getCapabilities().commandIntents is present and every RuntimeCommandIntentId key ('compact') maps to a { supported: boolean } object; (b) assert that a runtime declaring commandIntents.compact.supported === true yields a compact_boundary (or a terminal event) from executeCommandIntent('compact'), and a runtime declaring it false throws when executeCommandIntent is called. In Phase 1 every production runtime is supported:false with a throwing placeholder (tasks 1.2/1.3), so the 'unsupported throws' branch is asserted for them and passes; FakeAgentRuntime is supported:true with a real synthetic boundary, so the 'supported yields boundary' branch is asserted and passes. In Phase 2, when claude-code/opencode/test-mode flip to supported:true with real bodies, this SAME suite re-asserts the supported branch for them — no conformance edit needed in Phase 2. Add a purpose comment to each new assertion; no always-pass tests. Files: packages/test-utils/src/runtime-conformance.ts (and re-run any per-runtime conformance test that already calls runtimeConformance). Acceptance: conformance runs green against FakeAgentRuntime and every production runtime's existing conformance test. Verify: pnpm vitest run packages/test-utils/src/__tests__/fake-agent-runtime.test.ts and the runtime conformance test files under apps/server. Cites: spec §Testing Strategy (Runtime conformance). Depends on 1.2 (caps field) and 1.3 (method).

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P2] claude-code executeCommandIntent('compact') real body + enable capability
tasks.description: Implement claude-code's real compact fulfillment and enable its capability. In the claude-code adapter (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/ — the runtime class that carried the placeholder from task 1.3) implement executeCommandIntent(sessionId, 'compact', opts?) to send the bare '/compact' prompt through the adapter's existing SDK send path (the same path sendMessage uses), reusing DOR-107's bare-passthrough + the getKnownCommands cache wired at claude-code-runtime.ts:294, and yield the turn's StreamEvents. No new Claude-SDK surface — it wraps the shipped '/compact' mechanism; the @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk import stays confined to this directory (Hard Rule 2). In the same change flip CLAUDE_CODE_CAPABILITIES.commandIntents.compact to { supported: true } (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/runtime-constants.ts) so the wired body and the capability land together. Do NOT touch trigger-turn.ts or the DOR-107 guard in message-sender.ts:343-377 — they stay unchanged (spec Acceptance Criteria). Files: apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/*. Acceptance: server typecheck passes; claude-code conformance (now supported:true) asserts a compact_boundary/terminal event from executeCommandIntent and passes. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/__tests__ (the conformance test for this runtime). Cites: spec §Detailed Design 3 (claude-code). Gated on Phase 1 (1.4, 1.5); runs in parallel with 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 and all of Phase 3.

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P2] opencode executeCommandIntent('compact') via session.summarize + enable capability
tasks.description: Implement opencode's real compact fulfillment and enable its capability. In the opencode adapter (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/) implement executeCommandIntent(sessionId, 'compact', opts?): resolve the session's opencode ses_* id, then call client.session.summarize({ path: { id: ocSessionId } }) — @opencode-ai/sdk import confined to this directory (Hard Rule 2); omit the body (providerID/modelID) so compaction uses the session's own model. The compaction is reported out-of-band as session.compacted, and the existing event-mapper (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/event-mapper.ts:239) already maps it to operation_progress done + compact_boundary on the session stream, so the generator yields nothing (or a single synthetic ack) — the boundary arrives via the global event hub. In the same change flip OPENCODE_CAPABILITIES.commandIntents.compact to { supported: true } (runtime-constants.ts). Files: apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/*. Acceptance: server typecheck passes; opencode conformance (supported:true) passes per the conformance contract (a terminal/boundary event observed for the compact path). Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/__tests__. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 3 (opencode), §Technical Dependencies (@opencode-ai/sdk@1.17.13 Session.summarize). Gated on Phase 1 (1.4, 1.5); parallel with 2.1, 2.3, 2.4 and Phase 3.

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P2] Finalize codex (honest-disabled throw) + test-mode (synthetic boundary) bodies
tasks.description: Finalize the two remaining runtime bodies. (a) codex (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/): replace the task-1.3 placeholder with the FINAL defensive behavior — executeCommandIntent throws a typed 'unsupported' error; CODEX_CAPABILITIES.commandIntents.compact stays { supported: false } (Thread.run only, no compaction/summarize API in @openai/codex-sdk@0.142.5 — verified). It is never reached because the route gates on supported===false, but the throw is the correct defensive contract. (b) test-mode (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/): replace the placeholder with a real async generator that yields a synthetic compact_boundary so e2e (Phase 4) and conformance can assert dispatch reached the runtime; flip TEST_MODE_CAPABILITIES.commandIntents.compact to { supported: true }. Files: apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/*, apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/*. Acceptance: server typecheck passes; codex conformance asserts the unsupported-throws branch, test-mode conformance (now supported:true) asserts a compact_boundary. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/__tests__ apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/__tests__. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 3 (codex, test-mode), §2 caps matrix. Gated on Phase 1 (1.4, 1.5); parallel with 2.1, 2.2, 2.4 and Phase 3.
tasks.activeForm: Finalizing codex and test-mode command-intent bodies

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P2] POST command-intents route + triggerCommandIntent projector wiring + OpenAPI + DirectTransport
tasks.description: Add the server route and in-process path that drive a runtime-fulfilled intent through the durable session projector. Route (a new thin apps/server/src/routes/command-intents.ts, or extend routes/sessions.ts; follow the flat-routes + thin-handler rule in .claude/rules/server-structure.md): POST /api/sessions/:id/command-intents/:intent — validate :intent against RuntimeCommandIntentId with Zod (unknown → 422), resolve the session's runtime via runtimeRegistry, check getCapabilities().commandIntents[intent].supported (unsupported → honest 409/422 error, adapter NOT called), else drive runtime.executeCommandIntent through the same durable-projector + session-lock lifecycle used for turns and return 202 (trigger-only; the client learns of the compaction via GET /api/sessions/:id/events). Reuse the triggerTurn projector + lock machinery in apps/server/src/services/session/ (trigger-turn.ts, session-state-projector.ts, session-lock.ts) via a thin triggerCommandIntent sibling — it does not need canonical-id rekey or context assembly. Register the route in the app route table (apps/server/src/app.ts if a new file) and in the OpenAPI registry (apps/server/src/services/core/openapi-registry.ts — add a registerPath entry mirroring the /api/sessions/{id}/messages 202 trigger entry at ~line 415, with 202/409/422 responses). Finally wire DirectTransport.runCommandIntent (apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct-transport.ts, placeholder from task 1.4) to call the same triggerCommandIntent service in-process, modeled on embedded-turn-trigger.ts. Files: apps/server/src/routes/command-intents.ts (or sessions.ts), apps/server/src/services/session/trigger-command-intent.ts, apps/server/src/services/core/openapi-registry.ts, apps/server/src/app.ts, apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct-transport.ts. Acceptance: server + client typecheck pass; the route appears in /api/openapi.json; the openapi-fresh check is green. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 3 (projector wiring) + 4 (Transport + route), ADR-0264. Gated on Phase 1 (1.4, 1.5); parallel with 2.1, 2.2, 2.3.
tasks.activeForm: Building the command-intent route and projector wiring

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P2] Server route tests (supported → 202+events, unsupported → error, unknown → 422)
tasks.description: Add server route + integration tests for the command-intent path. Create apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/command-intents.test.ts using FakeAgentRuntime + scenarios from @dorkos/test-utils and SSE assertions via collectDurableEvents (mirror routes/__tests__/sessions-events.test.ts). Cases (each a purpose comment): a supported runtime → POST returns 202, executeCommandIntent is driven, and the resulting events (compact_boundary / terminal) reach the durable projector and are observable on the events stream (collectDurableEvents); an unsupported runtime → honest 409/422 error and the adapter's executeCommandIntent is NOT called; an unknown :intent (e.g. 'frobnicate') → 422. No always-pass tests. Files: apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/command-intents.test.ts. Acceptance: all cases pass. Verify: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/command-intents.test.ts. Cites: spec §Testing Strategy (Server — route). Depends on 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 (needs the real route + real bodies + honest caps).

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P3] Inline palette: intent entries + dedupe by canonical token AND alias + alias-hint reuse
tasks.description: Project the shared command-intent registry into the inline slash palette as one row per intent, deduping the native runtime command by canonical token OR any alias. In apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx (the allCommands merge at ~lines 221-225) build INTENT_ENTRIES by mapping COMMAND_INTENTS (from '@dorkos/shared/command-intents') into CommandEntry rows — each row carries the intent's aliases (CommandEntry.aliases) so the shipped ranker (entities/command/lib/rank-command.ts) and the shipped 'matched /{alias}' hint (features/commands/ui/CommandPalette.tsx:89-93) light up for free. Merge order: [...INTENT_ENTRIES, ...NATIVE_COMMAND_ENTRIES, ...runtimeCommands] where runtimeCommands is the registry list filtered so any command whose '/'-prefixed token is in commandIntentTokens() OR in the native token set is dropped. Today the merge dedupes only by token — extend it to dedupe by canonical token AND any alias (e.g. Claude's SDK '/usage' must fold into the context intent, not double). Keep ChatPanel thin: if the INTENT_ENTRIES builder grows, extract it to a small helper module in features/chat/model or entities/command. Tests (add/extend the chat + features/commands unit tests, each with a purpose comment): an intent entry deduping a colliding native runtime command by token AND by alias; the 'matched /{alias}' hint renders on an alias query. Files: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx (+ optional INTENT_ENTRIES helper), test files. Acceptance: palette shows exactly one row per intent, no duplicates, alias hint on match; client typecheck + tests pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run <new/updated palette test path>. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 5a (palette source), VC2. Gated on Phase 1 (1.4, 1.5); parallel with 3.3 and all of Phase 2.
tasks.activeForm: Deduping the palette and adding intent entries

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P3] Honest capability gating: disabled 'Not supported by {runtime}' palette row
tasks.description: Gate the runtime-fulfilled intent honestly in the palette. The palette reads the active runtime's capabilities via the shipped useCapabilitiesForRuntime (apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/model/use-runtime-capabilities.ts). A runtime-fulfilled intent (fulfillment === 'runtime', i.e. compact) whose commandIntents[id].supported === false renders DISABLED with the reason 'Not supported by {runtime}'; client-native intents (clear/context) are always enabled. In apps/client/src/layers/features/commands/ui/CommandPalette.tsx add a disabled-row visual style + a non-selectable state that keyboard navigation skips (respect the existing keyboard nav). Pass the supported/reason props onto the compact intent row from the palette source (ChatPanel), derived from the active runtime caps. Microcopy follows the writing-for-humans skill. Tests (purpose comment each): a disabled row renders for an unsupported runtime-fulfilled intent and keyboard nav skips it (mock useCapabilitiesForRuntime to return compact.supported=false); an enabled row for a supported runtime. Files: apps/client/src/layers/features/commands/ui/CommandPalette.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx, test files. Acceptance: disabled 'Not supported by Codex' row on codex; enabled on claude-code/opencode; keyboard nav skips disabled; client typecheck + tests pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run <CommandPalette gating test path>. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 5b (honest gating), VC3. Depends on 3.1 (shared palette source; gates on Phase 1 transitively).
tasks.activeForm: Adding honest capability gating to the palette

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P3] clear + context native executors + NativeCommandContext capabilities
tasks.description: Add clear and context to the client-side native-command seam (ADR-0300) as local executors that never reach the runtime, and extend NativeCommandContext with the two capabilities they need. In apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/native-commands/registry.ts add two NATIVE_COMMANDS entries alongside 'rename': (1) clear — executor calls ctx.startFreshSession(sessionId): create a fresh session in the SAME project/cwd and navigate to it via the use-session-id setter (→ /session?session=<id>), reusing the existing new-session flow; no message is sent, no model turn. DECOMPOSE DECISION (continuedFrom link): the spec forbids a SQLite schema change, so record the prior session id ONLY via the existing session store/create path if it already accepts optional metadata; otherwise keep 'continuedFrom' as client navigation state (do NOT add a DB column). (2) context — executor calls ctx.focusUsageSurface(): reveal/pin the DOR-100 usage & cost detail (the same content as the UsageStatusItem tooltip — apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/UsageStatusItem.tsx in ChatStatusSection, fed by streamValues.usage: UsageStatus) in a focused/pinned popover so a keyboard user who typed /context sees utilization + cost without hovering; when the session has no usage yet (hasRenderableUsage false, e.g. a cold codex session) show the honest empty state 'No usage data for this session yet.'; no message sent. Extend NativeCommandContext (registry.ts) with startFreshSession(fromSessionId: string | null): void and focusUsageSurface(): void, and inject both from use-native-commands.ts — startFreshSession reuses the new-session flow + navigation; focusUsageSurface toggles a minimal client-only reveal state for the usage popover (add a small signal/store in features/chat or features/status if needed). Both executors return { handled: true, ran: true }. Tests (native-commands/__tests__, purpose comment each): clear calls startFreshSession + navigates and sends no message; context calls focusUsageSurface; both return handled+ran; the honest empty-state renders when hasRenderableUsage is false. Files: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/native-commands/registry.ts, .../use-native-commands.ts, apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/UsageStatusItem.tsx (reveal hook if needed), native-commands/__tests__. Acceptance: /clear opens a fresh linked session and /context focuses the usage surface, both identical on all runtimes with no model turn; typecheck + tests pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/native-commands/__tests__. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 5c (clear + context native), Operator Decision 2, Acceptance Criteria (clear/context). Gated on Phase 1 (1.4, 1.5); parallel with 3.1 and Phase 2. NOTE: if this exceeds one focused pass, split cleanly along the two executors (clear vs context).
tasks.activeForm: Building the clear and context native executors

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P3] compact recognition + runCommandIntent dispatch + honest unsupported toast
tasks.description: Recognize the runtime-fulfilled compact intent in the existing client interception funnel and dispatch it, with an honest unsupported path. In apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-submit.ts (executeSubmission) and apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-chat-queue.ts (handleQueue) — the same funnel the native-command seam already uses — recognize a runtime-fulfilled intent via resolveCommandIntent (from '@dorkos/shared/command-intents'). When matched AND supported by the active runtime (useCapabilitiesForRuntime → commandIntents.compact.supported === true): call transport.runCommandIntent(sessionId, 'compact'), clear the composer, and do NOT POST a message. When matched but UNSUPPORTED: notify 'Compact isn't supported by {runtime}' (toast) and KEEP the composer text — never send it as literal text to the model (resolves the silent-failure regression). Keep a single client-side recognition point for all three canonical intents (clear/context branch to their native executors from task 3.3; only compact branches to runCommandIntent). This stays inside features/chat/model — the shared registry crosses as a package import, not an FSD layer edge (no violation). Tests (chat model __tests__, purpose comment each): compact recognition calls transport.runCommandIntent when supported and does not POST; when unsupported it calls notify and does NOT send-as-text (mock useCapabilitiesForRuntime + transport). Files: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-submit.ts, .../use-chat-queue.ts, test files. Acceptance: /compress or /summarize on a supported runtime triggers runCommandIntent + clears the composer; on codex it toasts unsupported + keeps the text; typecheck + tests pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run <use-session-submit / use-chat-queue test paths>. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 5d (compact dispatch), VC1, Acceptance Criteria (unsupported never sent as text). Depends on 3.3 (shared interception funnel; gates on Phase 1 including Transport.runCommandIntent transitively).

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P4] E2E: palette dedupe + honest gating across runtimes
tasks.description: Add a Playwright e2e (apps/e2e) verifying palette dedupe + honest gating in the live cockpit, backed by the test-mode runtime (its caps declare compact supported after Phase 2 task 2.3). Follow the browser-testing skill + existing apps/e2e patterns. On a claude-code (or test-mode-supported) session, open the inline '/' palette and assert exactly one '/compact' row with an alias hint (type an alias like '/compress' and assert the 'matched /{alias}' hint). Switch to a codex session and assert the '/compact' row is disabled with 'Not supported by Codex' and that keyboard nav skips it. Files: apps/e2e/<command-intents spec>. Acceptance: e2e passes headless. Verify: the apps/e2e Playwright run for the new spec (pnpm --filter @dorkos/e2e test <spec>, or the repo's browsertest entry point). Cites: spec §Testing Strategy (E2E), VC2 + VC3. Depends on 2.5 (server route working), 3.2 (gating) and 3.4 (dispatch). Priority medium — spec marks e2e optional-but-recommended.
tasks.activeForm: Writing the palette dedupe + gating e2e

tasks.subject: [universal-command-intents] [P4] Docs microcopy pass + #133 doc reconciliation
tasks.description: Cross-cutting docs + housekeeping. (a) Optional-but-recommended: add a short user-facing slash-command reference entry in docs/ for the three intents (compact/clear/context) now that the native set is three-plus, following the writing-for-humans skill (describe what happens for the user, plain language); if a new doc page is added, register it in contributing/INDEX.md and confirm docs:coverage stays green. (b) Confirm all shipped UI microcopy — palette descriptions, disabled reason 'Not supported by {runtime}', the unsupported toast, and the honest 'No usage data for this session yet.' empty state — reads plainly per writing-for-humans. (c) Reconcile the stale specs/sdk-command-discovery/04-implementation.md (#133) status to 'superseded' or 'implemented' (housekeeping flagged in ideation; not gating) and update its manifest entry if present. Files: docs/<slash-commands reference>.mdx (if added), contributing/INDEX.md (if a doc is added), specs/sdk-command-discovery/04-implementation.md (+ manifest). Acceptance: docs build/coverage green if a page is added; #133 status reconciled. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build (if docs changed) or the docs:coverage check. Cites: spec §Documentation. Depends on 3.4 (so microcopy reflects shipped strings). Priority low.
